medium_tasks_dataset.description: Users complain about weekend payments, but standard metrics look fine.
initial_messages: Why is it only failing for weekend events?

analyze_failure_pattern.log_impact: Pattern identified: Failures correlate heavily with weekend timestamps.
analyze_failure_pattern.hint: Investigate logs deeper to find the exact edge case condition.
user_messages: Support queue seems slightly better, but some weekend events still failing.
analyze_failure_pattern.else_hint: You already found the pattern. Move on to root cause analysis.

identify_edge_case_condition.log_impact: Root cause found: leap-year/weekend logic flaw in date parser.
identify_edge_case_condition.hint: A fix is now possible. Apply the patch.
user_messages: Wait, I think the errors stopped increasing.
identify_edge_case_condition.else_hint: You need to analyze the failure pattern first, or you've already found it.

user_messages: Everything is working perfectly now! Thanks!
apply_transaction_fix.else_hint: You must identify the root cause before blindly applying a fix.

inspect_transaction_logs.log_impact: Reviewed transaction logs. Some date parsing anomalies detected.
inspect_transaction_logs.hint: Logs show anomalies. Try to identify the specific edge case condition.
inspect_transaction_logs.else_hint: You already have the root cause. Stop reading logs.

restart_payment_service.hint: Restarting without diagnosing did not help. The logic bug remains.
user_messages: The system went down for a minute, and the weekend bug is STILL there!

medium_tasks_dataset.description: Users complain the app becomes unresponsive when they export large reports.
medium_tasks_dataset.initial_logs: [WARN] Memory usage critically high. App performance degraded.
initial_messages: The site freezes completely when I click 'Export to PDF'.
It was fine this morning, now it's unusable.
Takes 5 minutes to load the dashboard!

check_monitoring_dashboards.log_impact: Dashboard shows massive memory spikes correlating with PDF export requests.
check_monitoring_dashboards.hint: You need to profile the memory to see exactly what object is leaking.

profile_memory_allocation.log_impact: Heap dump analyzed. 'PDFRenderer' objects are never being garbage collected.
profile_memory_allocation.hint: The leak source is found. You can now patch the exporter.
user_messages: Seems a bit faster right now, maybe people stopped exporting?
profile_memory_allocation.else_hint: Apply the patch, you know what the issue is.

patch_pdf_exporter_leak.log_impact: PDF Exporter patched. Garbage collection is now clearing the renderer objects.
user_messages: Exporting works instantly now, and the site is super fast. Great job!
patch_pdf_exporter_leak.else_hint: You can't patch what you haven't identified. Profile the memory first.

restart_app_servers.hint: Restarting clears the memory temporarily, but the leak will return. Find the root cause.
user_messages: It's fast again! Wait, no, it's slowing down already...
